content.text: Let me start by reading the file and checking for available formatters.

content.content:
1→{
     2→  config,
     3→  pkgs,
     4→  lib,
     5→  ...
     6→}:
     7→
     8→{
     9→  imports = [
    10→    ./hypridle.nix
    11→    ./hyprlock.nix
    12→    ./hyprpaper.nix
    13→    ./mako.nix
    14→    ./waybar.nix
    15→  ];
    16→
    17→  home.packages = with pkgs; [
    18→    grim # Screenshot tool
    19→    slurp # Region selector
    20→    swappy # Snapshot editor
    21→    wl-clipboard # Clipboard manager
    22→    wf-recorder # Screen recorder
    23→    ffmpeg # For GIF conversion
    24→    jq # JSON processor
    25→
    26→    # Script to record GIF
    27→    (pkgs.writeShellScriptBin "record-gif" ''
    28→      #!/usr/bin/env bash
    29→      # Define output
    30→      DATE=$(date +%Y-%m-%d_%H-%M-%S)
    31→      DIR="$HOME/Pictures/GIFs"
    32→      MKV="$DIR/recording_$DATE.mkv"
    33→      GIF="$DIR/recording_$DATE.gif"
    34→
    35→      mkdir -p "$DIR"
    36→
    37→      # Notify and Select Region
    38→      notify-send "GIF Recording" "Select region to start recording..."
    39→
    40→      if ! REGION=$(slurp); then
    41→          notify-send "GIF Recording" "Cancelled"
    42→          exit 1
    43→      fi
    44→
    45→      notify-send "GIF Recording" "Recording started! Press Super+Shift+S to stop."
    46→
    47→      # Record to MKV (more robust than MP4 for interruption)
    48→      wf-recorder -g "$REGION" -f "$MKV"
    49→
    50→      # Convert to GIF after recording stops
    51→      notify-send "GIF Recording" "Converting to GIF..."
    52→
    53→      # Generate palette for better quality
    54→      palette="/tmp/palette.png"
    55→      # higher quality scale
    56→      filters="fps=15,scale=720:-1:flags=lanczos"
    57→
    58→      ffmpeg -v warning -i "$MKV" -vf "$filters,palettegen" -y "$palette"
    59→      ffmpeg -v warning -i "$MKV" -i "$palette" -lavfi "$filters [x]; [x][1:v] paletteuse" -y "$GIF"
    60→
    61→      rm "$palette"
    62→      rm "$MKV"
    63→
    64→      notify-send "GIF Recording" "Saved to $GIF"
    65→      wl-copy "$GIF"
    66→    '')
    67→
    68→    # Screenshot scripts (avoids quoting issues in Hyprland binds)
    69→    (pkgs.writeShellScriptBin "screenshot-region" ''
    70→      REGION=$(slurp) || exit 0
    71→      grim -g "$REGION" - | wl-copy
    72→    '')
    73→    (pkgs.writeShellScriptBin "screenshot-region-edit" ''
    74→      REGION=$(slurp) || exit 0
    75→      grim -g "$REGION" - | swappy -f -
    76→    '')
    77→
    78→    # Cheatsheet Script
    79→    (pkgs.writeShellScriptBin "hypr-cheatsheet" ''
    80→      #!/usr/bin/env bash
    81→      # Parse keybindings from hyprctl using structured JSON
    82→      hyprctl binds -j | jq -r '
    83→        .[]
    84→        | select(.has_description and .description != "")
    85→        | (
    86→            (if .modmask == 0 then ""
    87→             else [
    88→               (if (.modmask % 128) >= 64 then "Super" else empty end),
    89→               (if (.modmask % 2) >= 1 then "Shift" else empty end),
    90→               (if (.modmask % 8) >= 4 then "Ctrl" else empty end),
    91→               (if (.modmask % 16) >= 8 then "Alt" else empty end)
    92→             ] | join(" + ")
    93→             end)
    94→          ) as $mods
    95→        | (if $mods == "" then .key else $mods + " + " + .key end)
    96→          + " — " + .description
    97→      ' | fuzzel --dmenu --width 80 --lines 20 --prompt "Keybindings: "
    98→    '')
    99→  ];
   100→
   101→  wayland.windowManager.hyprland = {
   102→    enable = true;
   103→    settings = {
   104→      # --- Monitors ---
   105→      # Auto-detect monitors with preferred resolution
   106→      # Format: name, resolution, position, scale
   107→      monitor = [
   108→        "DP-2, highrr, auto, 1"
   109→        ", preferred, auto, 1"
   110→      ];
   111→
   112→      # --- Workspace Layouts ---
   113→      workspace = [
   114→        "1, layout:scrolling"
   115→        "2, layout:master"
   116→        "3, layout:master"
   117→        "4, layout:master"
   118→        "5, layout:dwindle"
   119→      ];
   120→
   121→      # --- Input ---
   122→      input = {
   123→        kb_layout = "us";
   124→        follow_mouse = 1;
   125→        sensitivity = 0; # -1.0 - 1.0, 0 means no modification.
   126→        natural_scroll = true;
   127→      };
   128→
   129→      # --- General ---
   130→      general = {
   131→        gaps_in = 5;
   132→        gaps_out = 10;
   133→        border_size = 2;
   134→        "col.active_border" = "rgb(cba6f7) rgb(94e2d5) 45deg"; # Catppuccin Mocha Mauve -> Teal
   135→        "col.inactive_border" = "rgb(585b70)"; # Catppuccin Mocha Surface2
   136→        layout = "master";
   137→      };
   138→
   139→      # --- Master Layout ---
   140→      master = {
   141→        new_status = "slave";
   142→        orientation = "left";
   143→        mfact = 0.55;
   144→      };
   145→
   146→      # --- Decoration ---
   147→      decoration = {
   148→        rounding = 10;
   149→        blur = {
   150→          enabled = true;
   151→          size = 3;
   152→          passes = 1;
   153→        };
   154→        shadow = {
   155→          enabled = true;
   156→          range = 4;
   157→          render_power = 3;
   158→          color = "rgba(1e1e2eee)"; # Catppuccin Mocha Base
   159→        };
   160→      };
   161→
   162→      # --- Cursor ---
   163→      cursor = {
   164→        no_hardware_cursors = true;
   165→      };
   166→
   167→      # --- Layer Rules ---
   168→      layerrule = [
   169→        "blur on, ignore_alpha 1, match:namespace waybar"
   170→        "blur on, ignore_alpha 1, match:namespace notifications"
   171→      ];
   172→
   173→      # --- Window Rules ---
   174→      # Hide Chrome's Spotify "now playing" floating popup (redundant with waybar mpris)
   175→      windowrule = [
   176→        "match:class ^(google-chrome)$, match:float yes, match:title .*•.*, workspace special:trash silent"
   177→      ];
   178→
   179→      # --- Keybindings ---
   180→      # NOTE: Keyboard is a Kinesis Advantage (split ergo). Super, Enter, Space,
   181→      # Ctrl(R), PgUp, PgDn are all on the right thumb cluster. Avoid chords
   182→      # combining any two of these keys (e.g. Super+Return, Super+Space).
   183→      # Super+Backspace works (Backspace is on the left thumb cluster).
   184→      #
   185→      # Directional home row keys: J=left, K=down, L=up, ;=right
   186→      # (Kinesis Advantage key wells make JKL; more natural than HJKL)
   187→      #
   188→      # Modifier scheme for JKL;:
   189→      #   Super       = focus (spatial, works across all layouts)
   190→      #   Super+Shift = move/swap window
   191→      #   Super+Ctrl  = resize
   192→      #   Super+Alt   = group/tab cycling
   193→      "$mainMod" = "SUPER";
   194→      bindd = [
   195→        "$mainMod, Q, Open terminal, exec, kitty"
   196→        "$mainMod, C, Close window, killactive,"
   197→        "$mainMod, M, Lock screen, exec, hyprlock"
   198→        "$mainMod, E, File manager, exec, dolphin"
   199→        "$mainMod, Y, Terminal file manager, exec, kitty -e yazi"
   200→        "$mainMod, B, Open browser, exec, google-chrome-stable"
   201→        "$mainMod, V, Toggle floating, togglefloating,"
   202→        "$mainMod, F, Toggle fullscreen, fullscreen, 1"
   203→        "$mainMod SHIFT, F, True fullscreen, fullscreen, 0"
   204→        "$mainMod, R, App launcher, exec, fuzzel"
   205→        "$mainMod, slash, Keybindings cheatsheet, exec, hypr-cheatsheet"
   206→        "$mainMod, P, Pseudotile, pseudo,"
   207→
   208→        # Focus
   209→        "$mainMod, left, Focus left, movefocus, l"
   210→        "$mainMod, right, Focus right, movefocus, r"
   211→        "$mainMod, up, Focus up, movefocus, u"
   212→        "$mainMod, down, Focus down, movefocus, d"
   213→
   214→        # Focus (home row — spatial movefocus works across all layouts)
   215→        "$mainMod, J, Focus left, movefocus, l"
   216→        "$mainMod, K, Focus down, movefocus, d"
   217→        "$mainMod, L, Focus up, movefocus, u"
   218→        "$mainMod, semicolon, Focus right, movefocus, r"
   219→
   220→        # Move window
   221→        "$mainMod SHIFT, left, Move window left, movewindow, l"
   222→        "$mainMod SHIFT, right, Move window right, movewindow, r"
   223→        "$mainMod SHIFT, up, Move window up, movewindow, u"
   224→        "$mainMod SHIFT, down, Move window down, movewindow, d"
   225→
   226→        # Move window (home row)
   227→        "$mainMod SHIFT, J, Move window left, movewindow, l"
   228→        "$mainMod SHIFT, K, Move window down, movewindow, d"
   229→        "$mainMod SHIFT, L, Move window up, movewindow, u"
   230→        "$mainMod SHIFT, semicolon, Move window right, movewindow, r"
   231→
   232→        # Master layout
   233→        "$mainMod, BackSpace, Swap with master, layoutmsg, swapwithmaster master"
   234→        "$mainMod SHIFT, BackSpace, Focus master, layoutmsg, focusmaster auto"
   235→        "$mainMod, comma, Add master window, layoutmsg, addmaster"
   236→        "$mainMod, period, Remove master window, layoutmsg, removemaster"
   237→
   238→        # Scroll layout (workspace 1)
   239→        "$mainMod, equal, Fit visible columns, layoutmsg, fit visible"
   240→        "$mainMod SHIFT, equal, Cycle column width, layoutmsg, colresize +conf"
   241→
   242→        # Workspaces
   243→        "$mainMod, 1, Workspace 1, workspace, 1"
   244→        "$mainMod, 2, Workspace 2, workspace, 2"
   245→        "$mainMod, 3, Workspace 3, workspace, 3"
   246→        "$mainMod, 4, Workspace 4, workspace, 4"
   247→        "$mainMod, 5, Workspace 5, workspace, 5"
   248→
   249→        # Move to workspace
   250→        "$mainMod SHIFT, 1, Move to workspace 1, movetoworkspace, 1"
   251→        "$mainMod SHIFT, 2, Move to workspace 2, movetoworkspace, 2"
   252→        "$mainMod SHIFT, 3, Move to workspace 3, movetoworkspace, 3"
   253→        "$mainMod SHIFT, 4, Move to workspace 4, movetoworkspace, 4"
   254→        "$mainMod SHIFT, 5, Move to workspace 5, movetoworkspace, 5"
   255→
   256→        # Screenshots
   257→        ", Print, Screenshot region to clipboard, exec, screenshot-region"
   258→        "$mainMod, Print, Screenshot region to editor, exec, screenshot-region-edit"
   259→        "SHIFT, Print, Screenshot fullscreen, exec, grim - | wl-copy"
   260→
   261→        # Screenshot alternatives (no Print key)
   262→        "$mainMod SHIFT, P, Screenshot region to clipboard, exec, screenshot-region"
   263→        "$mainMod ALT, P, Screenshot region to editor, exec, screenshot-region-edit"
   264→        "$mainMod CTRL, P, Screenshot fullscreen, exec, grim - | wl-copy"
   265→
   266→        # Screen recording
   267→        "$mainMod SHIFT, G, Record GIF, exec, record-gif"
   268→        "$mainMod SHIFT, S, Stop recording, exec, pkill --signal SIGINT wf-recorder"
   269→
   270→        # Workspace cycling
   271→        "$mainMod, Tab, Next workspace, workspace, m+1"
   272→        "$mainMod SHIFT, Tab, Previous workspace, workspace, m-1"
   273→
   274→        # Urgent
   275→        "$mainMod, U, Focus urgent window, focusurgentorlast,"
   276→
   277→        # Groups (tabbed windows)
   278→        "$mainMod, G, Toggle group, togglegroup,"
   279→        "$mainMod ALT, J, Change active in group backward, changegroupactive, b"
   280→        "$mainMod ALT, semicolon, Change active in group forward, changegroupactive, f"
   281→
   282→        # Notifications
   283→        "$mainMod SHIFT, N, Restore notification, exec, makoctl restore"
   284→        "$mainMod, N, Dismiss notification, exec, makoctl dismiss"
   285→      ];
   286→
   287→      # --- Mouse Bindings ---
   288→      bindm = [
   289→        "ALT, mouse:272, movewindow"
   290→        "ALT, mouse:273, resizewindow"
   291→      ];
   292→
   293→      # --- Keyboard Resize (Super+Ctrl+JKL;) ---
   294→      binde = [
   295→        "$mainMod CTRL, J, resizeactive, -20 0"
   296→        "$mainMod CTRL, K, resizeactive, 0 20"
   297→        "$mainMod CTRL, L, resizeactive, 0 -20"
   298→        "$mainMod CTRL, semicolon, resizeactive, 20 0"
   299→      ];
   300→
   301→      debug = {
   302→        disable_logs = false;
   303→      };
   304→    };
   305→  };
   306→
   307→  systemd.user.targets.hyprland-session = {
   308→    Unit = {
   309→      Description = lib.mkForce "Hyprland session";
   310→      BindsTo = [ "graphical-session.target" ];
   311→      Wants = [ "graphical-session.target" ];
   312→      After = [ "graphical-session.target" ];
   313→    };
   314→  };
   315→}
   316→

content.thinking:
Now I have the file contents. Let me make the changes:

1. Add a `scrolling` section after the `master` section
2. Add the Chrome window rule to the existing windowrule list

Then format with `nix fmt`.

Add scrolling layout config for 3-column ultrawide and Chrome width rule

Configure Hyprland scrolling layout with 0.333 default column width for
3-column ultrawide displays, with useful width presets. Add window rule
giving Chrome a slightly wider 0.4 default column.
EOF
)"

input.command:
gh pr create --title "Add scrolling layout config for 3-column ultrawide" --body "$(cat <<'EOF'
## Summary
- Add `scrolling` section to Hyprland settings with `column_width = 0.333` for 3-column default on ultrawide, and `explicit_column_widths` presets for convenient width cycling
- Add window rule giving Chrome a wider default column (0.4) so it gets extra space in a 3-column layout

## Test plan
- [x] `nix fmt` passes with no changes
- [x] `nix flake check` passes
- [ ] Verify on ultrawide that workspace 1 (scrolling layout) defaults to 3 columns
- [ ] Verify Chrome opens at ~40% width, other windows at ~33%
- [ ] Verify `Super+=` (fit visible) and `Super+Shift+=` (cycle width) work with new presets

Run: 20260324-1812-e324
EOF
)"

content.text:
PR created: https://github.com/patflynn/cosmo/pull/378

Changes made to `home/hyprland.nix`:
- Added `scrolling` section with `column_width = 0.333` and `explicit_column_widths` presets for ultrawide 3-column use
- Added window rule giving Chrome a 0.4 default scrolling width

Both `nix fmt` and `nix flake check` pass cleanly.
